---
title: "Prevalence and incidence Codes"
output: html_document
---



```{r}
# installing the required packages


install.packages("tidyverse") # for data management and wranging 
install.packages("here") # for directory path way 
install.packages("rio") # import and exporting data set
install.packages("janitor") # for cleaning data set


library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(rio)
library(janitor)


```


## loading data set

```{r}

# loading cholera data set 

cholera_data <- import(here("data", "cholera_data_2022.csv")) |> 
  clean_names() |> 
  mutate(date=as.Date(date, format="%Y-%m-%d"))


# loading mid year population data set
mw_population <- import(here("data", "mw_population.csv")) |> 
  clean_names()


```


## Prevalence proportion

```{r}
# Prevalence proportion

# This code will show the calculation of prevalence proportion using Malawi cholera-2022

# cleaning the district names in both data sets to have similar format

cholera_data$district <- str_to_lower(cholera_data$district)

mw_population$district <- str_to_lower(mw_population$district)


# aggregating the cases for first quarter (January to March) 2022
first_quarter <-cholera_data |> 
  filter(date >= "2022-01-01" & date <= "2022-06-30") |> 
  group_by(district) |> 
  summarise(first_quarter_cases=sum(cases, na.rm = TRUE))


# merging the two quarters data of cases columns
first_quarter<- merge(first_quarter, mw_population, by="district")


# calculating prevalence proportion for first quarter for each district

prev_district<- first_quarter |> 
  mutate(prevalence=round((first_quarter_cases/population)*10000,2))


# view the prevalence proportion for each district
print(prev_district)


```


##Incidence Proportion

```{r}

blantyre_incidence<- cholera_data |> 
  filter(district== "blantyre" ) |>
  filter(date >= "2022-01-01" & date <= "2022-06-30") |>
  group_by(district, epiweek) |>
  summarise(first_quarter_cases=sum(cases, na.rm = TRUE))


# merging with population data set
blantyre_incidence <- merge(blantyre_incidence, mw_population, by="district") |> 
  mutate(incidence_prop=round((first_quarter_cases/population)*100000,2)) |> 
  select( district, epiweek, incidence_prop) 

# viewing incidence proportion for Blantyre district per week
blantyre_incidence
  

```



## Incidence Rate

```{r}

Total_days_obs <- 816*21 # 816 days observed for 21 people
Years_obs <- ceiling(Total_days_obs/365)  # The assumption is not a leap year
Incidence_rate <-  (5/Years_obs)*10
print(Incidence_rate) 

```


# Risk Ratio (RR)

```{r}

install.packages("epitools")  # installing the epitools package

## load the epitools package. 
library(epitools) 
library(dplyr)


# create a data frame with two columns vaccine_state and disease_outcome
vaccine_data <- data.frame(
 vaccine_state= sample(rep(c("Vaccinated", "Unvaccinated"), 20)),   
 disease_outcome= sample(rep(c("Positive", "Negative"), 20)) # 
# print out the first 6 values from the data set created will use the head () function

)

# to print first 6 values
head(vaccine_data)

# calculating  the risk ratio, the following code line will be used 
riskratio(vaccine_data$vaccine_state, vaccine_data$disease_outcome)



# calculate the odds ratio 
oddsratio(vaccine_data$vaccine_state, vaccine_data$disease_outcome)

```


# Epidemic curve

```{r}

install.packages("lubridate")  # installing lubridate package for date manipulation

library(lubridate)


# Convert date to proper format and create epidemiological week using floor_date
# Epidemiological weeks start on Monday
cholera_data <- cholera_data %>%
  mutate(
    date = as.Date(date),
    epi_week_start = floor_date(date, unit = "week", week_start = 1)  # 1 = Monday
  ) %>%
  group_by(epi_week_start) %>%
  summarise(
    total_cases = sum(cases, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_deaths = sum(deaths, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_date = min(date, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_date = max(date, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  arrange(epi_week_start)

# Define the scaling factors for the dual axes
# Primary axis: 0-5000 (cases)
# Secondary axis: 0-150 (deaths), with scale factor of 50
scale_factor <- 150 / 5000  # Converting deaths scale to cases scale

# Create the plot with dates on x-axis
p <- ggplot(cholera_data, aes(x = epi_week_start)) +
  # Column bars for cases (primary axis)
  geom_col(aes(y = total_cases), fill = "#FFA500", color = "#FF8C00", alpha = 0.8, width = 5) +
  # Line plot for deaths (needs scaling for secondary axis)
  geom_line(aes(y = total_deaths / scale_factor), color = "#FF4500", linewidth = 1.2) +
  # Primary y-axis (cases)
  scale_y_continuous(
    name = "Cases",
    limits = c(0, 5000),
    breaks = seq(0, 5000, by = 1000),
    sec.axis = sec_axis(
      ~ . * scale_factor,
      name = "Deaths",
      breaks = seq(0, 150, by = 50)
    )
  ) +
  # X-axis with date formatting (by month)
  scale_x_date(
    date_breaks = "1 month",
    date_labels = "%Y-%m-%d",
    expand = c(0.01, 0)
  ) +
  # Formatting
  labs(
    title = "Weekly Epi curve of cholera cases in Malawi March 2022 to July 2024",
    x = "Epidemiological Week",
    y = "Cases"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title.y = element_text(color = "black", size = 10),
    axis.title.y.right = element_text(color = "black", size = 10),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
  )

# view the plot
print(p)


```
